Title: Promiscuous CYP87A enzyme activity initiates cardenolide biosynthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Foxglove; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Digitalis purpurea; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in young and old leaves of D. purpurea; low in roots/leaf base
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In D. purpurea, highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family 87 subfamily A (CYP87A); ER-localized plant CYP inferred
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts sterol substrates including cholesterol and campesterol, and likely also β-sitosterol, to pregnenolone through side-chain cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step from sterol precursor to pregnane intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed first step; upstream entry point
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the committed precursor of cardenolides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as first committed step and key entry point to the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane (proposed/inferred for plant sterol-biosynthetic CYP context)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; human therapeutic use including treatment of congenital heart failure, atrial arrhythmia, cancers and other chronic diseases
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stable isotope pregnenolone feeding; greenhouse/growth-room conditions; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA929980; metabolite profiling (LC-MS, GC-MS)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified from D. purpurea transcriptome as a leaf-enriched CYP candidate correlated with cardenolide accumulation. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana produced pregnenolone and reduced cholesterol, campesterol and β-sitosterol. 3. Co-expression with AtCPR1 in sterol-engineered yeast converted cholesterol or campesterol to pregnenolone. 4. Stable overexpression in Arabidopsis caused de novo pregnenolone accumulation. 5. RNAi silencing in D. purpurea abolished detectable pregnenolone and strongly reduced cardenolides, confirming direct pathway role.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pregnenolone formation and is required for normal cardenolide accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; heterologous expression in engineered yeast; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis; RNAi knockdown in D. purpurea; GC-MS/LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point enzyme for engineering sustainable production of cardenolides and other high-value steroids in heterologous hosts; suitable for pathway reconstruction and synthetic biology
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi lines showed strong depletion of cardenolides; overexpression caused ectopic pregnenolone accumulation, but broader fitness effects not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Silencing increased campesterol without major cholesterol change, suggesting campesterol may be the preferred precursor in D. purpurea; Ile210 is important for catalytic activity/substrate binding; mechanism likely analogous to mammalian CYP11A1 three-step chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Calotropis procera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Calotropis procera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in stem and roots, negligible in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. procera, highest in stem and roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts cholesterol and campesterol, and likely β-sitosterol, to pregnenolone through sterol side-chain cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step from sterol precursor to pregnane intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed first step; upstream entry point
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the committed precursor of cardenolides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as first committed step and key entry point to the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; human therapeutic use of related cardenolides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young leaves in C. procera, while expression is highest in stem and roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous expression in yeast; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1003839; metabolite profiling (LC-MS, GC-MS)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the C. procera homologue of DpCYP87A106 by BLAST despite poor correlation between transcript abundance and leaf cardenolide levels. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana produced pregnenolone and lowered cholesterol, campesterol and β-sitosterol. 3. Co-expression with AtCPR1 in cholesterol- and campesterol-producing yeast yielded pregnenolone. 4. Stable expression in Arabidopsis produced de novo pregnenolone. 5. Results suggest early cardenolide biosynthesis may occur in C. procera stem/roots with transport of pregnenolone or downstream intermediates to leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pregnenolone formation as the pathway entry step toward cardenolides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; heterologous expression in engineered yeast; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis; GC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Tool enzyme for reconstructing pregnenolone/cardenolide biosynthesis and synthetic biology production of steroidal metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct plant fitness tradeoff reported; expression pattern suggests tissue-specific transport complexity for engineering in native context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Spatial mismatch between gene expression and cardenolide accumulation implies transport of pregnenolone or downstream intermediates in C. procera; Ile209 is important for activity/substrate binding

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Putative sterol side-chain cleavage cytochrome P450
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transient expression increased pregnenolone accumulation, suggesting conversion of sterol substrate(s) to pregnenolone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely endogenous sterols in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step from sterol precursor to pregnane intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established beyond likely analogous entry-step role
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; pharmacological value of related cardenolides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified from phylogenetic survey of CYP87A proteins in cardenolide-producing and cardenolide-free plants. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana increased pregnenolone accumulation. 3. Suggested to participate in Erysimum cardenolide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes pregnenolone formation and contributes to cardenolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential additional enzyme source for synthetic biology of pregnenolone/cardenolide production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is more limited than for DpCYP87A106 and CpCYP87A103; proposed to have arisen by duplication and neofunctionalization from an EcCYP87A2-like ancestor
